entry.title: SQL learning
entry.content:
• Why (Structure Query Language)SQL?
    • High level
    • Highly compatible
  • key & superkey & foreign key
    • Primary Key
    • Unique Key
    • Candidate Key
    • Alternate Key
    • Composite Key
    • Super Key
    • Foreign Key
    • what is a key
    • foreign key
      • constraint
    • integrity constraint
  • catgorize
    • L
    • R
    • LR
    • N
  • DDL
    • table schema
    • table
    • view & temporary table
  • DML
    • insert
    • delete
    • update
  • QL
  • commands
    • SELECT
    • WHERE
    • AS
    • IN = ANY
    • LIKE
    • BETWEEN
    • ORDER BY
      • Order the result set by the value of a specific column
      • When the specific column has same value, a secondary column will be helpful
    • INSERT
      • Add a complete new row to the table
      • Add a partial row in to the table
    • UPDATE
    • DELETE
  • Advanced SQL
    • ROUND
    • list set bag
    • commands
      • DISTINCT
      • GROUP BY
      • HAVING
      • SUBSTRING()
      • || or CONCAT
      • DAYDIFF() (MySQL only)
      • INTO
      • INSERT INTO
      • LIMIT
      • INTERSECT
      • UNION
      • ALL
  • problems when using
    • time zone not correct

自学网站 https://sqlzoo.net/

Why (Structure Query Language)SQL?#

High level#

As a high level language you only need to care what data you want from the DBS rather then how I’m going to get it or what algorithm I’m going to use.

Highly compatible#

With only have to write what you need in SQL query and let DBS to decide how to get the results. Scientists can add new algorithm in to the DBS. And you don’t have to make any change to your SQL queries.

key & superkey & foreign key#

Primary Key#

Primary Key is a field that can be used to identify all the tuples uniquely in the database. Only one of the columns can be declared as a primary key. A Primary Key can not have a NULL value.

Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_id” is the Primary Key as it can identify all the row uniquely from the table.

Unique Key#

Unique Key can be a field or set of fields that can be used to uniquely identify the tuple from the database. One or more fields can be declared as a unique Key. The unique Key column can also hold the NULL value. Use of Unique Key improves the performance of data retrieval. It makes searching for records from the database much more faster & efficient.

Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_aadhaar_number”, “cust_pan_number” are the Unique Key as it can allow one value as a NULL in the column

Candidate Key#

Candidate Key can be a column or group of columns that can qualify for the Unique Key. Every table has at least one Candidate Key. A table may have one or more Candidate Key. Each Candidate Key can work as a Primary Key if required in certain scenarios.

Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_id”, “cust_aadhaar_number”, “cust_pan_number” are the Candidate Key as it can identify all the row uniquely from the table. These columns also qualify the criteria to be a Primary Key.

Alternate Key#

Alternate Key is that Key which can be used as a Primary Key if required. Alternate Key also qualifies to be a Primary Key but for the time being, It is not the Primary Key.

Example: In the above given relational table, “cust_aadhaar_number”, “cust_pan_number” are the Alternate Key as both of the columns can be a Primary Key but not yet selected for the Primary Key.

Composite Key#

Composite Key is also known as Compound Key / Concatenated Key. Composite Key refers to a group of two or more columns that can be used to identify a tuple from the table uniquely. A group of the column in combination with each other can identify a row uniquely but a single column of that group doesn’t promise to identify the row uniquely.

Example: In the above given relational table i.e. Order Table, “cust_id”, “order_month_year” group of these columns used in combination to identify the tuple uniquely in the Order Table. The individual column of this table is not able to identify the tuple uniquely from the Order table.

Super Key#

Super Key is a combination of columns, each column of the table remains dependent on it. Super Key may have some more columns in the group which may or may not be necessary to identify the tuple uniquely from the table. Candidate Key is the subset of the Super Key. Candidate Key is also known as minimal Super Key.

Example: In the above given relational table, Primary Key, Candidate Key & Unique Key is the Super Key. As a single column of Customer Table i.e ‘cust_id’ is sufficient to identify the tuples uniquely from the table. Any set of the column which contains ‘cust_aadhaar_number’, ‘cust_pan_number’ is a Super Key.

Foreign Key#

A foreign key is a column which is known as Primary Key in the other table i.e. A Primary Key in a table can be referred to as a Foreign Key in another table. Foreign Key may have duplicate & NULL values if it is defined to accept NULL values.

Example: In the above given relational table, ‘cust_id’ is Primary Key in the Customer table but ‘cust_id’ in the Order table known as a ‘Foreign Key’. Foreign Key in a table always becomes the Primary Key on the other table.

what is a superkey#

the key that can make sure $superkey1 \ne superkey2 \rightarrow tuple1 \ne tuple2$

what is a key#

proper subset of superkey

foreign key#

reference of other(or itself) table’s key

constraint#

can only add to table if the foreign key is exsit in it’s reference table

integrity constraint#

primary domain, and some rules that it have to obay

catgorize#

L#

need to be include in primary key

R#

never needed to be include in primary key

LR#

sometimes need to be include in primary key

N#

DDL#

CREATE TABLE Books(BookNo INTEGER NOT NULL UNIQUE, SortNo CHAR(10), Title CHAR(30), Author CHAR(12), Price FLOAT, LoanNo INTEGER);
VARCHAR(32); #fixed lengthCHAR; #variable lengthINTEGER;SMALLINT;NUMERIC; #precision & scaleDECIMAL;DATE(year, month, day);TIME(hour, minute, second);NOT NULL can be use to force data input when adding a new tuple, so if a column have no exist or currently unknown is not convenient

table schema#

relation no null or duplicate allowed

table#

ALTER TABLE BooksADD new_col data_type constraint;DROP;ALTER COLUMN col_name data_type;
DROP TABLE table_name [RESTRICT|CASCADE]
UNIQUE(); # except for null no duplicatePRIMARY KEY(); # only one for each table, NOT NULL for every attribute from the list
FOREIGN  KEY(attribute-list) REFERENCES(table attribute-list)ON DELETE SET NULLON DELETE

if there is a null in a simple semantic, it will be ignore
and in pasha(strict) semantic

so we can choose strict or simple in DBMS like mysql or postgre?

view & temporary table#

view are a pre-produce table, that comes from a existing table, which can save storage

CREATE ASSERTION name CHECKCREATE VIEW ASCREATE GLOBAL TEMPORARY TABLECREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY TABLE

DML#

manipulating language

insert#

INSERT INTO nameVALUES value-listINSERT INTOVALUES INSERT INTO

delete#

DELETE FROM nameWHERE condition

update#

UPDATE name SET set-nameWHERE condition

QL#

query language

commands#

SELECT#

SELECT col_name1(,) (col_name2...)FROM tbl_name;

The table like this contains only requested columns is called result set.

idnametype_1type_2
1BulbasaurGrassPoison
2IvysaurGrassPoison
3VenusaurGrassPoison

DON’T use SELECT * in production data

  1. Unnecessary cost.
  2. Two table return same column name might lead to a crash.

WHERE#

SELECT col_name1(,) (col_name2...)FROM tbl_nameWHERE condition;

The condition is used to evaluate every row in the table. When a row is evaluated true, it will be contain in the result set.

  • Conditions
  1. =
  2. >=
  3. <=
  4. >
  5. <
  6. <> not equal, sometimes != works too
  7. AND between two operators
  8. NOT pre-fix
  9. OR

AS#

SELECT col_name1(,) AS name1 (col_name2...)FROM tbl_nameWHERE condition;

AS can change the column name into alias in the result set.
If you want to use space in a alias you need to use ‘’ to quota it. However, in MySQL “” is also compatible.

IN = ANY#

Test if the value inside column matches any one item in a list of values.

SELECT col_name1FROM tbl_nameWHERE col_name1 IN ('A', 'B', ...);

There can be more than this in the brackets, it can be a result set generate by another query.

LIKE#

Just like = but wildcart can be use

SELECT col_name1FROM tbl_nameWHERE col_name1 LIKE '%a';
  • Wildcards
SQLRegex
%.*
_.

BETWEEN#

Restrain the value in a range

SELECT col_name1, col_name2FROM tbl_nameWHERE col_name1 BETWEEN 20 AND 30;
  • Does BETWEEN A AND B have to satisfied $A<B$ ? yes

ORDER BY#

Order the result set by the value of a specific column#

SELECT *FROM tbl_nameORDER BY col_name ASC;

By default it will be sort in ascending order, if you prefer descending order, add DESC after the col_name

When the specific column has same value, a secondary column will be helpful#

SELECT *FROM tbl_nameORDER BY col_name1 ASC, col_name2 DESC;

this secondary won’t be any help unless there are rows that have same value in first column

INSERT#

Add a complete new row to the table#

  • for a table like this
idnamecountrylanguage
1JohnUSEnglish
2SamUKEnglish
3TomCANFrench
INSERT INTO tbl_nameVALUES (4, 'Chen', 'Chinese');

A query like this will produce a table like this will cause a error column doesn’t match, cause there are 4 columns in table and only 3 values given in the query.

INSERT INTO tbl_nameVALUES (4, 'Chen', 'CN', 'Chinese');

A correct one like this turn the table into

idnamecountrylanguage
1JohnUSEnglish
2SamUKEnglish
3TomCANFrench
4ChenCNChinese

Add a partial row in to the table#

For the last table, command like this

INSERT INTO tbl(id, name, language)VALUES (5, 'Yoki', 'Janpaness');

this will produce a table as follow

idnamecountrylanguage
1JohnUSEnglish
2SamUKEnglish
3TomCANFrench
4ChenCNChinese
5YokiNULLJapaneses

Or the NULL could be the value you defined as DEFAULT at the beginning.

UPDATE#

Modify existing rows

UPDATE tableSET col_name1 = 'rabbit'WHERE col_name2 = 1;

DELETE#

Delete data from a table

==Caution:==DELETE FROM tbl_name; will delete all the data in the table!

Advanced SQL#

ROUND#

ROUND(a,b)

b needs to be a integer but it can be under 0 which means to round into 10’s

list set bag#

Features\typelistsetbag
duplicateyesyesyes
orderingfixedunorderedno

commands#

DISTINCT#

Remove duplicates

GROUP BY#

When there are columns you SELECT has different row numbers, it will get confused which to print. So this is when you use GROUP BY
GROUP BY is to group rows into different subset base on column(s), and do aggregates against each subset.

HAVING#

==Caution:== Can’t use an aggregation in a WHERE filtering clause, because they are not yet computed.
By using a HAVING clause instead can use an uncomputed aggregation for filtering.

SUBSTRING()#

SUBSTRING(value, a, b) will get the a-b part of the value, cut a part of the string. The count start at 0. So for example SUBSTRING('suggestions', 0, 5) is equal to 'sugges'.

|| or CONCAT#

str_1 || str_2 will concatnate str_1 and str_2

CONCAT() in MySQL works the same. Or in MySQL just put 'str_1' 'str_2' together, MySQL will concatenate it automatically

DAYDIFF() (MySQL only)#

Caculate how many days are there from day A to day B.

to do this in sqlite is to do

SELECT CAST((julianday(day_A) - julianday(day B)) AS INT) AS days

The date format is ‘yyyy-mm-dd’

INTO#

SELECT DISTINCT col_name INTO newtableFROM table;

Copy column(s) into a new table.

CREATE TABEL newtable(SELECT DISTINCT col_name FROM table;)

In MySQL this will do the same thing.

INSERT INTO#

INSERT INTO table(SELECT DISTINCT col_name FROM table;)

Copy column(s) into exsiting table.
==caution:== the column number must match

LIMIT#

LIMIT 20 OFFSET 10

Skip 10 and return 20 rows(11~30)

INTERSECT#

the intersect of two select results And

UNION#

the union of two select results Or

ALL#

All the rows in the inner query must satisfy the expression

problems when using#

time zone not correct#

SHOW VARIABLES LIKE '%time_zone%';#show current time zone setting by default is UTC SYSTEMset global time_zone = '+8:00';#this will change the global time_zone varible, it will take effect in next sessionset time_zone = '+8:00';#this only change the varible in this sessionflush privileges;#save the changes

entry.title: UNIX basic commands
entry.content:
基础命令#

lsmkdir rmrmdircd touchcatechogrep|>>>ln (-s) find <起始目录> <选项表达式> <条件匹配表达式> <动作表达式># 选项表达式-L 遇到符号链接，跟踪链接地址-regextype (-regex/-iregex)-depth/-d-moount/-xdev 查找时不跨越文件系统-maxdepth LEVEL 最大查找深度# 条件匹配表达式-namemyfile'*myfile*'-iname 文件名含有-amin access N minutes ago-atime access N days ago-cmin create N minutes ago-ctime create N days ago-mmin modify N minutes ago-mtime modify N days ago#    访问文件：vim/sh/bash/cat/more等操作会重置Access time#    读取文件属性：ls/stat/wc/du等操作不会重置Access time#    修改文件：会重置Modify time 和 Change time#    修改权限但不调整内容： 不会重置Modify time， 但会重置 Change time。-used used in N minutes-newer-uid-gid-user -group-nouser 所属不在/etc/passwd-nogroup 所属组不在/etc/group-perm MODE 权限为MODE-perm -MODE 权限至少为MODE-perm /MODE或+MODE 有一个用户权限为MODE即可-type b c d p f l s-links N 硬链接数量-inum N inode为N-samefile NAME 查找与NAME文件具有相同inode的文件-size-empty#动作表达式-print-ls-prune-exec COMMAND {} \;-ok COMMAND {} \;# 用户管理adduseraddgroupusermod# 网络ifconfig# down load a url/*wget -m -np -nH

entry.title: shell basic
entry.content:
Job Control#

stop#

  • Ctrl-C (cancel SIGINT)
  • Ctrl-\ (SIGQUIT)
  • kill

pause#

  • Ctrl-Z (SIGSTOP)
  • fg
  • bg (still children of current terminal)
  • & suffix (run a job in background and print all the output which can be annoying)
  • nohup (start a job that live beyond the terminal)
  • disown (not longer own one job, so it won’t be kill when you exit the terminal)

use return value as a part of latter command#

outer command \ inner command`

Command editing#

  • !$ last argument
  • !:1 the first argument from last command
  • !:1-2
  • Alt + . look for previous arguments

entry.title: linux可以访问硬盘的情况下修改密码
UNIX cli tools comparing
entry.content:
• 搜索
    • fg
  • 结果整理
    • rg
    • sed
    • awk
    • cut
  • 比较
    • diff
    • cmp
    • sdiff
    • wdiff
  • 网络工具
    • ifconfig
    • mntui

搜索#

find#

  • 实时扫描

fg#

  • 比find更快，用数据库，类似于windows下的everything

结果整理#

rg#

  • 搜索工具,比grep快,可以搜索流或者文件内容

sed#

  • 按正则匹配筛选

awk#

  • 按字符块输出, 支持正则筛选

cut#

比较#

diff#

  • 比较文件或目录

    cmp#

  • 比较两个文件并且定位每个不同的位置

    sdiff#

  • 比较文件并且可以合并

    wdiff#

  • 一个单词一个单词比较不同

网络工具#

ifconfig#

  • ifconfig eth0(device) 192.168.10.2(given ip) netmask 255.255.255.0 broadcast 192.168.10.255 up(turn on)

    mntui#

  • 命令行ui，适合在没有DE的情况下配置wifi，依赖NetworkManager。

entry.title: ubuntu power saving
entry.content:
优化原理#

优化cpu调度#

tlp#

使用tlpui
AC/BAT调度模式分别设置为schedutil和powersave
关闭BAT状态下的boost
AC/BAT性能策略为default/balance_power

禁用不必要服务项及应用#

systemctl #查看所有服务项  systemctl #stop 查看临时禁用是否有影响  systemctl #disable 禁用开机启动  systemctl #mask 阻止关联启动  kill $(pidof {the programe you want to stop})# 我禁用了compton(窗口渲染器)

限制进程cpu资源分配#

  • cpulimit
    cpulimit -e {nameOfProgram} -l 30# -l 的最大值是cpu线程数

entry.title: learn to use git
entry.content:
git

[toc]

Jargon#

  • Tree
    folder

  • Blob
    file

  • Working tree
    Where all files exist

dose it contains files that is not added?

  • Staging area(Index)
    What added and are going to be contain in next commit will be in the staging area

  • Repository
    Database that stores the history of the project

basic use#

Tree status#

  1. Dirty/modified
    None of the modified files have been added for next commit

  2. Staged
    Some of the files have been added and can be commit now

  3. Committed
    Change have been stored safely in a snapshot(commit)

File status#

  1. Tracked
    Were part of last snapshot or newly added to the stage

    • modified: have changed but not yet added
    • unmodified: haven’t changed
    • staged: have changed and add to stage or newly added to the stage

  2. Untracked
    Not part of last snapshot nor are in the staging area

commands#

git commit#

git commit -a

include all the tracked files including modified but not staged ones

git commit -m

add a commit message in the commit command rather than use a default editor to write one

git stash#

To store dirty files in a special stack

git stash

to store all dirty files

git stash list

show previous stashes

git stash apply

apply latest stash

git stash apply stash@{num}

apply specific stash

git stash drop

same as apply only clear the stash instead of applying

git stash pop

same as apply only drop it after apply it

git stash clear

clear all the stashes

git merge#

Merge two branches

git fetch#

To get the remote branches that not on local repository

git pull#

Combination of git fetch and git merge

git push#

Push a branch to remote

git push <remote> <local branch>:<rename as>

push a brach to remote but use a different name

git branch#

git branch -u <remote>/<remote branch>

set up to stream with remote branch

git rebase#

Redo the changes made to one branch since the branching to the another branch. This will make the changes are made in series not in parallel

git remote#

git remote add origin [git@url:port]:path/project.git# change the remote HEAD of origin to develop branch git remote set-head origin develop# set default push branchgit branch --set-upstream-to=origin/mac

git checkout#

git checkout change into the situation of a commit
-b create a new branch and change into it

git diff#

git diff diff a file between different commit

Branching change and merging#

If meeting merge conflict, try to automatically fix it, if can’t, report it.

entry.title: computer network
entry.content:
• 要学什么
  • internet 需要什么
  • jargon
    • what is the internet
    • network edge
    • network core
      • routing table
      • datagrame(udp)
  • computer network
    • packet
      • old time packets look like this.
      • new pockets
    • layering
      • feature
      • example
      • why layering?
    • encapsulate
      • upper layers don’t have to worry about what is the next layer.
      • example
      • compare VPN packet and a normal one
    • byte order
      • when programming a network application
    • ipv4
      • example
      • net mask
      • address structure
      • Classless Inter-Domain Routing(CIDR)
    • longest prefix match(algorithm)
    • ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)
    • TCP service model
      • reliable
      • end2end
      • bidirection
      • 3 hand shakes
      • transmit in segments
      • teardown the connection
      • summary
  • congestion control
    • timeout
    • 3 duplicate ACKs
    • TCP Tahoe always sets cwnd to 1 (timeout or 3 duplicate acks)
  • IP
    • SDN(software designed network)
    • ATM(面向连接的三层交换机)
    • router filter
    • MAC
      • multiple access links protocols
      • MAC address and ARP
      • ethernet
  • others
  • security
    • cryptography and its many uses beyond “confidentiality”
    • authentication
    • message integrity
    • availability
    • firewalls and intrusion detection systems
    • security in application, transport, network, link layers
  • review
    • 都是大题

要学什么#

  • 电子设备中间传递信息
  • 怎么实现

internet 需要什么#

  • 设施
  • 服务
  • 协议

jargon#

英文中文
transmission网卡放01代码速度
host(end systems)终端
switch交换机（通常指数据链路层交换机）
router路由器（网络层）
path=route 路径
package switch把传输数据分段后发送
secret switch
transmission rate
protocol语义 语法 时序
format, syntax(order), actions taken
frame网卡实现package到frame
package软件封包
Web
VoIP
email
games
e-commerce
social nets
client
server
physical media传输媒介
network corerouters, switch, network of networks
core switch
distribution switch
access switch
ITU国际电信联盟
RFCRequest For Comment 协议认定组织
IEDF
ISPInternet Service Providers
TCPTransmission Control Protocol
UDPUser Datagram Protocol
ICMPInternet Control Message Protocol (ping)
IPInternet Protocol
SMTPSimple Mail Transfer Protocol
SNMPSimple Network Management Protocol
IMAPInternet Message Access Protocol
POPPost Office Protocol
HTTPHyper Text Transfer Protocol
DNSDomain Name System protocol
FTPFile Transport Protocol
DHCPDynamic Host Configuration Protocol
ARP/RARPAddress Resolution Protocol
RIP
PPP

sends packet of data
主要受限因素，网卡数据转化01速度，网线传输时间忽略不计
packet transmission delay = time needed to transmit L-bit packet into link = $\frac{L(bits)}{R(bits/sec)}$

  • delay comes from transmission time consuming and queueing time consuming
  • lost comes from no buffer in any hop

switch 只关心物理和数据链路层
router 关心物理层数据链路层和网络层

what is the internet#

network edge#

network core#

  • mesh of interconnected routers
  • packet-switching: store-and-forward

routing table#

  • base on routing algorithms to calculate the dynamic routing table

PDU | protocol data unit|协议头加内容

datagrame(udp)#

  1. 无连接
  2. 不可靠
  3. 顺序无法保障

computer network#

packet#

old time packets look like this.#

contentheader
xxxxxxxdes C B A
  • The data transport form source to A, and when A find B is the next hop in the header and send it to B.
  • Eventually the packet will be sent to the destination.
  • And since all the hops are list in the packet that every router can see it, that is unsafe. So they come up with a new one.

new pockets#

  • When shake hands and open connection, all the routers will record where to send if the destination is this one in a table like this.
destinationwhere to send
desB
  • So the pockets will only contains the destination.
  • And when router receive a pocket it go look up it in the table and sent it to the next hop.

layering#

feature#

  • only communicate with the layer above or below.
  • a massage goes form the toppest layer(application layer) all the way to the bottom(physic layer), and then gose all the way to the top again to reach the destination.

example#

  • edit (source code) compile (object code) link (executable) execute

why layering?#

  1. modularity
  2. well defined service
  3. reuse
  4. separation of concerns
  5. continuous improvement
  6. p2p communications

encapsulate#

upper layers don’t have to worry about what is the next layer.#

example#

  • when the tcp change it’s algorism http don’t need to worry about how to change into tcp, they can work both on the old tcp and the new one.

compare VPN packet and a normal one#

  • wireless frame/ip/tcp/tls/ip/tcp/http
  • wireless frame/ip/tcp/http

byte order#

byte orderbig endiansmall endian
orderthe first byte place in the first addressthe last byte place in the first address
use in what cpuamd intelarm

when programming a network application#

  • remember how many time you convert or you will be facing lots of bugs.

ipv4#

  • 32 bit long

example#

  • a.b.c.d (each letter represent a number from 0-255 which is an octect of binary number)

    an octect is a 8 digits number

net mask#

  • which ranger of ip are local that don’t need to go through a router
  • when you send a package and whenever the netmask is 1 the corresponding binary digits of you ip address and desitination address are the same you are in the same local network.

address structure#

  • Class A: first bit is 0 and next 7 bit is network and last 24 is hosts
  • Class B: first 2 bit is 1 0 and next 14 bit is network and last 16 is hosts
  • Class C: first 3 bit is 1 1 0 and next 21 bit is network and last 8 is hosts

Classless Inter-Domain Routing(CIDR)#

  • use slash and a number to descibe the length of the prefix(netmask)
  • /16 means the netmask is 16 bit of binary number which left 16 bit which is 65536 ipv4 addresses under the domain.
  • similarly, /24 describes 256 addresses and /20 describes 4096 address.

longest prefix match(algorithm)#

  • it is a algorithm to decide how to forward a packet
  • if a packet arrives and there isn’t a more specific match in the forwarding table than the default one, it will sent it through the default one.
  • forwarding table is a set of CIDR entries. an address can match multiple entries. base on prefix length to decide which is more specific.

ARP (Address Resolution Protocol)#

  • only works in local network
  • every node keep a cache of mapping of IP addresses to the link layer addresses(MAC).
  1. When the node need to send a packet to an IP address:
    • If it has it in the cache it send it directly.(jump to 4)
    • If it does not have the cache it will broadcast a request that ask “who have IP x.x.x.x tell x.x.x.x(itself) at xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx(MAC)”
  2. The node that have the specific IP will respond “I hve network address x.x.x.x and my link layer addresses is xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx”(not broadcast). And to the rest nodes in the network when they overhear the request, they can insert or refresh their ARP mapping in the cache.
  3. The node that send the request will generate a mapping of link layer addresses and IP addresses.

TCP service model#

reliable#

  • Make sure every segment is well received without missing.
  • Flow-control prevents overrunning receiver.(tells the sender how many room in receiver’s buffer)

end2end#

bidirection#

3 hand shakes#

  1. SYN (synchronize)
    • Send along with a number that identified where to start the byte-stream.
  2. SYN+ACK (acknowledge)
    • Acknowledge of agreeing to communicate with it. And also send a SYN back for reverse direction connection.
  3. ACK
    • Acknowledge of agreeing to the reverse direction connection.

SYN#

  • synchorize the data

ACK#

  • ACK=n means the packages before n is received and ready to receive n

retransmission#

  1. timeout
  2. duplicates ACKs(3 duplicates) will trigger retransmission

transmit in segments#

teardown the connection#

FIN (finish)#

  • No longer have data to send.

(Data)+ACK#

  • Send the data before closing. And acknowledge that you are going to close the connection.

FIN#

  • When finish the rest of data that need to transmit, send a FIN to tell that I have done sending data. You can close now.

ACK#

  • The connection is now closed.

ACK, checksum, sequence-number

summary#

  1. e2e reliable data transfer
  2. syntax
  3. time order
  4. communitive ack
  5. flow control
  6. congestion control
  7. 3 hand shake & 4 hand wave

Unit 3 home work:
R 1,7,8,11,14,15,18
P 3,7,8,18,26,31,40,44

congestion control#

timeout#

cwnd set to 1 MSS

3 duplicate ACKs#

cut in half

TCP Tahoe always sets cwnd to 1 (timeout or 3 duplicate acks)#

3.51

IP#

SDN(software designed network)#

A central cluster automatically calculate the best ip table for each router and continuously renew them.

ATM(面向连接的三层交换机)#

router filter#

  1. high-speed switching fabric(硬件交换，不需要处理数据，类似PLC)
    二进制位比较，先去掉掩码长度的头部，遍历选择最优路线（匹配位数最长的出口）

MAC#

multiple access links protocols#

p2p ppp

collision(碰撞) simultaneous transmission by nodes on single shared broadcast channel

Master Slave, 轮询

pass token one by one, who got the token can use the channel

Random compete the channel

switch store and forwarding

MAC address and ARP#

MAC address is 64bit

ARP is a protocol, know ip, want to know MAC
RARP = reverse ARP
ARP table is MAC to IP

波特率（每秒可以传输多少码元）/带宽

ethernet#

preamble 7 bytes of 10101010 followed by one 10101011

others#

  • CRC 校验(used in MAC layer)
  • multiple access protocols

security#

cryptography and its many uses beyond “confidentiality”#

f4 并行算法

authentication#

message integrity#

availability#

firewalls and intrusion detection systems#

firewall: filter application layer, transportation layer, network layer, link layer
IDS: analyze data

security in application, transport, network, link layers#

review#

  • 考前六章
  • 每个协议的内容：语义 语法 时序

    都是大题#

  • 问答题
  • 综合题
  • 算法题（dv， link-stat迪杰斯特拉）
  • 实验题（路由表，分配端口，ip，启动端口）
  • 体系结构（5层）
  • 6.7 web
  • encapsulate
  • 子网划分 因特网架构 （子网掩码，网段划分
  • 路由配置、算法
  • 距离向量算法（chapter 5 p256）
区别路由器交换机
工作层级网络层数据链路层
处理的协议数据单元（pdu）packagedata frame
局域网交换按照ip转发按照mac地址转发

entry.title: blockchain intro.md
entry.content:
基础理解#

术语理解
区块一段时间的账目
链每个区块之间联系起来的数据
PoW共识（挖矿）<br > 检查记账是否正确
网络节点矿工，记账先生是共识机制选出的特定的网络节点
P2P网络互相通信以保障区块的不可篡改
共识机制选举记账先生，给予记账先生手续费与报酬
分布式账本没有哪个是权威，共同维护数据可靠性

遥感数据存储#

IPFS#

IPFS#

Inter-planetary file system

分布式点对点文件系统，可以实现类似git的版本控制，网络会删除重复文件，并对每个文件进行版本历史追踪，每个文件都会生成对应的哈希值用于智能合约

好处#

  1. 易于分布：分布式文件存储协议，所以对于不同节点来说都是易于访问的
  2. ==仅需储存单实例即可保障安全性==（估计是类似与raid那种可以通过校验值还原数据）
  3. 低带宽需求：点对点传输，可以通过IPFS寻址共享数据，缩短带宽需求（不需要中转服务器），加快数据共享速度
  4. 归档：不可改变数据存储，安全性保障

安全#

目前常采用基于可信第三方的信任机制 （中心化）

分布式节点间的信任关系

服务商可以存在越权行为，滥用用户在云端的数据

基于区块链的PKI#

原有CA采用集中式PKI（公钥管理基础设施）#

  • 容易被中间人攻击
    非对称加密公钥发布过程无法确定是否是正确的目标对象发送的，可能是中间人发送的
  • 单点故障可能导致大规模证书的不能使用

基于公共总帐建立PKI能够建立分布式PKI#

  • 多点认证提高中间人攻击难度
  • 提高数据存放份数，避免单点故障问题
  • 证书签发透明

    实例Certcoin#

  • 每个区块的merkle root涵盖三个内容（identity, public key, action: registration/update/revocation）

    PB-PKI#

  • 基于区块链的PKI的改进
  • key-update方法与Certcoin不一样，PB-PKI不把之前的公钥和id上链

总结共识算法（优先），智能合约#

可以从医学数据、农产品了解

证明类#

工作量证明（PoW）#

权益证明（Proof of Stake，PoS）#

授权股权证明（DPoS）#

权威证明(Proof of Authority，PoA)#

贡献证明（Proof of Contribution，PoC）#

拜占庭类#

拜占庭算法 raft 不支持容错作恶节点，容错1/2#

实用拜占庭算法 pbft 支持容错作恶节点，容错小于1/3#

Algorand#

Tendermint#

燃烧证明、存在证明(Proof of Existence，PoE)[29]、数据可恢复证明（Proof of Retrievability，PoR）[30]、存储证明（Proof of Storage）[31]

DAG类#

IOTA#

Byteball#

结合云计算#

哈希值，追溯#

数据存储和分享#

医疗领域应用#

使用区块链安全地分享CT图像供深度学习训练使用

安全性#

本地进行模型训练，然后将权重通过智能合约分享

准确性#

使用==BAT算法==和==数据扩增==减少噪点和==过拟合==的出现

运作原理#

把图像进行本地模型训练，把权重信息储存在IPFS中，然后再把IPFS的hash值和权重值存进区块链账目中
深度学习处理本地模型数据，将处理后的权重存进IPFS，hash值存进区块链账目

https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/10.1007/s41870-020-00568-9.pdf

http://webvpn.swu.edu.cn/https/77726476706e69737468656265737421e7e056d234336155700b8ca891472636a6d29e640e/science/article/pii/S0895611120301075

entry.title: server building
entry.content:
• configurations
  • system installing
  • service running/been ran on it

configurations#

  • processor

i3-7100T
35w

Since servr have to run 7*24, it should be power-saving. Otherwise it would be a large eletricity bill to pay.

  • motherboard

昂达 B365SD3-ITX

This is really not the best motherboard to use, DDR3 runs at a lower speed and when compared to DDR4 it only have slight price adventage. But I have two 4g DDR3 RAM, so I choice this to suit with 7 gen cpu and DDR3 at the same time.

  • RAM

DDR3 4g * **2 镁光

  • power supply

300w

  • case

银欣ITX

  • cooling system

IDCOOLING IS-40X

4 heatpipe
PHANTEKS MG SKIRON 12cm

  • hard drive

2.5mm 2T Seagate SMR (quit using as soon as possible, when I can afford two PMR to build a raid array)

Toshiba 4T * 2 5400rpm (raid 1)

  • ssd

杂牌64G SATA（when SMART report it was danger to continue using, I change it into the Lexar I’m using now）

Lexar nm100 SATA 128G

system installing#

  1. Ubuntu 20.04LTS (place in the living room used both as a media player for my parents and a 24*7 was)

  2. OMV (when I found my parents seldom use it, I change into OMV for more convenient raid experience)

  3. FreeNAS (freeBSD need VM to run docker, when you are using a i3 processor it really drives people crazy)

  4. OMV (yes, I’m back)

  5. TrueNAS scale (useable ZFS+ Debian + docker, this is the dream NAS OS)

service running/been ran on it#

  1. Nextcloud (appreciate by my mum so she don’t need to use WeChat or flash drive to keep her documents synchronised)

  2. Jellyfin (use aria2 to download movie and Jellyfin to manage them is a good choice)

  3. No-ip (DDNS + Dynamic external IP is a solution to access it when you are at school, though expose you NAS can be danger for you data, so make sure you know what you are doing)

  4. PhotoPrime (google open-source gallery, use tenser flow to classify photos, after using iCloud this had been abandoned)

  5. Aria2 pro (best aria2 docker ever found, move when complete, auto update tracker-list and auto delete temp file when complete)
